content.problemStatement:
We call a binary array BBB of size MMM **winning** if it can be converted to the array [1,1,‚Ä¶,1][1, 1, \ldots, 1][1,1,‚Ä¶,1] by repeating the following operation a finite number of times:


Pick 1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§M1 \le L \le R \le M1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§M such that the number of 111's in the subarray B[L,R]B[L, R]B[L,R] is **strictly greater** than the number of 000's in it.

Then, replace BiB_iBi‚Äã with 111 for all L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R.
Given an array AAA of size NNN, count the number of **winning** subarrays of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN ‚Äî the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers ‚Äî A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of winning subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The following are the winning subarrays:

A[1,1]A[1, 1]A[1,1] : The array is [1][1][1]. All elements are already 111.
A[3,3]A[3, 3]A[3,3] : The array is [1][1][1]. All elements are already 111.
A[1,3]A[1, 3]A[1,3] : The array is [1,0,1][1, 0, 1][1,0,1]. We can do one operation with L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 and transform the array into [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1].
